core_mechanism: the eikonal/Hamilton-Jacobi transformation that recasts a linear wave field into a first-order flow of characteristic trajectories driven by the gradient of a complex action/phase field, whose global structure is organized by two generic singularity types of that flow: caustic (fold/envelope) singularities where neighbouring trajectories focus, and vortex (phase-winding branch-point) singularities where the field amplitude vanishes
brief_reason: The abstract analyzes interference through the complex quantum Hamilton-Jacobi formalism and states that the resulting trajectory topologies in the complex plane are ruled by two types of singularities, caustics (tied to free propagation) and vortices (tied to the interference/collision), which is exactly an action-gradient trajectory flow whose behaviour is governed by fold-envelope and phase-winding singularities.
